summary: Iteration 3 — re-tested the 2 frontend bugs from iteration 2. Both fixes verified PASSING: (1) /provvigioni page renders without React error, h1='Provvigioni', export-commissions-button visible and clicking it downloads provvigioni.csv. (2) <OfflineBanner/> is mounted globally in App.js (renders before BrowserRouter); banner appears with data-testid='offline-banner' and Italian copy 'modalità offline — sola lettura' both when window.dispatchEvent('offline') is fired AND when navigator is set offline before mount via context.set_offline(True)+reload. Pre-condition (banner hidden while online) also passes. Backend skipped per request.
critical_code_review_comments: Both prior issues correctly fixed: Commissions.jsx now imports Download from lucide-react and exportCommissions from ../utils/export; OfflineBanner.jsx now initialises show=!navigator.onLine at mount; App.js mounts <OfflineBanner /> globally before <BrowserRouter />.

Carry-over (still valid from iter1/iter2): server.py size keeps growing — split into routers; admin password not refreshed on env change; no brute-force lockout; sign endpoint accepts blank signature — should validate non-trivial signature payload.
Minor enhancement: OfflineBanner currently shows even on /login if offline at mount because it is outside BrowserRouter. That is acceptable UX (banner is global) but worth confirming with product.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (5/5 iter3 assertions: fix1_render, fix1_button, fix1_download, fix2_hidden_when_online, fix2_event, fix2_initial_offline)

seed_data_creation: None — used existing seed (4 commissions visible on /provvigioni: 2026-04 269,20€, 2026-04 499,50€ incassato, 2026-05 16,00€, 2026-05 40,00€).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 2 frontend bugs are RESOLVED. CSV download endpoint /api/commissions/export remains green (verified indirectly via the front-end download flow returning provvigioni.csv). OfflineBanner is mounted globally outside BrowserRouter so it appears on every route including /login. Backend pytest baselines from iter2 (test_p1_features.py, test_backend_api.py) were not re-run because the request was frontend-only.
rca of the issue: n/a — both bugs fixed and re-verified.
